design_input.inspection_note: Inspected live through the Figma MCP connector as David Revenaugh (Pro tier). node_id 0:1 remains the canonical token entry page; the document now spans 7 pages (see connector_receipt.document_pages). The MCP top-level page listing enumerates only page 0:1, so the remaining pages were inspected via their node-specific URLs.
design_input.authority: Figma is a controlled design input. DESIGN.md, the LifeOS Design System, and checked-in implementation contracts remain implementation authority.

document_pages.name: 00 — Overview + Tokens
document_pages.role: Design tokens, overview, file map, and the command-spine mental model.

document_pages.name: 01 — Components + Sidebar Spec
document_pages.role: Rail-button and detail-item component specs with states; 1440x900 expanded command-spine mockup; rail/active/command annotations.

document_pages.name: 01 — LifeOS Brand
document_pages.role: Canonical brand masters (lockups, wordmark, marks, triads), production usage, repository handoff mapping table, and purge notice.

document_pages.name: 02 — LifeOS Product Identity
document_pages.role: Brand architecture, product identity, ElementArk endorsement, navigation triad (work=cyan/personal=purple/home=green), usage documentation, repository export mapping, product-context proofs, and audit-evidence table.

document_pages.name: LifeOS / Screens
document_pages.role: Full application screen matrix (very large working canvas).

document_pages.name: LifeOS / Archive
document_pages.role: Archived LifeOS screens retained for history.

document_pages.name: Archive — Non-LifeOS Screens
document_pages.role: Archived non-LifeOS screens retained for history.

connector_receipt.document_pages_note: The Figma file's own 'File map' frame (node 5:37) still describes an aspirational three-page structure ('00 — Overview + Tokens', '01 — Components + Sidebar Spec', '02 — Responsive + Handoff'). The live document instead carries the seven pages above; '02 — Responsive + Handoff' was never built and '02 — LifeOS Product Identity' stands in its place. Recorded as an observation only; the Figma file is not edited from this repository.

component_variants.reason: Published component and variant inventory requires a Figma Dev or Full seat on an Organization or Enterprise plan.

code_connect.repository_approved_equivalent: This checked-in manifest, data-figma-component anchors, source mapping test, design-token verifier, and a11y/structural regression suites.
code_connect.upgrade_condition: When a qualified Figma seat is available, add official Code Connect mappings for the exact component nodes and retain this manifest as the repository-side receipt.

variables.live_check_2026_07_21: get_variable_defs on nodes 0:1 and 5:27 returned no bound variables. The companion documents tokens with local paint/text styles carrying a 'Sidebar Companion' prefix (see the 'Core tokens' caption), not bound Figma variables.
variables.disposition: No Figma variable is copied into source or treated as runtime authority.

typography.lifeos: Lexend for UI and Rigelstar only for designated display/wordmark roles
typography.relationship: intentional LifeOS Design System authority divergence

colors.relationship: nearest dark card surface; not a value import
semantic hover surface; LifeOS elevates to --bg-3 (--surface-card-hover) on hover while the companion darkens — LifeOS token authoritative

colors.swatch_anomaly: The Border swatch fill in the Figma file reads #69E438 (a green — a file-side glitch); the documented hex label #2A2A32 matches every card border and is the intended value recorded here.

spacing_and_shape.relationship: LifeOS compressed 4/6/8/12/16 scale remains authoritative; companion geometry is reviewed as input rather than copied.

icon_system.figma_disposition: Semantic icon roles map to Lucide names; no copied icon raster is authority.

figma_reference.node_name: Component Spec / Sidebar Detail Item / Default
figma_reference.spec_nodes: Detail item states 5:130–5:147 (Default/Hover/Active/Disabled/With badge); live items 5:191–5:224

upgrade_workflow: Inspect the exact file key and node through the Figma connector before changing source.
Refresh the connector receipt, component hierarchy, and token observations in this manifest; do not store a Figma screenshot as authority.
Compare each observed token against DESIGN.md and colors_and_type.css. Record an explicit design-contract decision for every divergence.
Update only the mapped Vue components and their interactive/a11y tests.
Run bun run figma:sidebar:check, bun run test:a11y, bun run test, and the visual/structural regression test.
If a qualified Figma seat becomes available, create and verify official Code Connect mappings for exact component nodes, then update code_connect status and receipts.

refresh_log.connector: Figma MCP (remote), authenticated David Revenaugh, Pro tier
changes: Corrected the truncated design_input.url mode flag (m=de -> m=dev).
Enumerated the live seven-page document inventory (connector_receipt.document_pages) via node-specific URLs, replacing the aspirational three-page declared_pages.
Reconciled observed colors to the full thirteen page-00 swatches, adding Hover (#18181B) and Border (#2A2A32) and recording the Border swatch-fill glitch.
Enriched the three command-spine component mappings with their page-01 (5:25) reference nodes.
Recorded the live get_variable_defs check (no bound Figma variables; local 'Sidebar Companion' paint/text styles instead).
Held Code Connect at seat_gated (Pro tier); no official Code Connect mapping is possible without an Org/Enterprise Dev seat.

prohibited_authority: Figma variables absent from the checked-in contract
